title: Question: Valgrind setup
body: --- Author Name: **Cheng Fei Phung** Original Redmine Message: 2307 from https://www.veripool.org --- I am having problem setting up valgrind in Makefile for https://github.com/promach/UART ``` TARGET=Tx .PHONY: $(TARGET) RTLSRC=../rtl all: $(TARGET) DESIGN_FILES=$(TARGET)_top.v TxUART.v baud_generator.v shift_register.v LDFLAGS= CFLAGS=-g -O3 DEBUG_ON = --debug --trace-dups --gdbbt VALGRIND_ON = $(DEBUG_ON) --gdb "valgrind -v --leak-check=yes" $(TARGET): $(PERL) verilator -Wno-fatal -I$(RTLSRC)/ $(VALGRIND_ON) --cc $(DESIGN_FILES) --trace --exe ../$(@).cpp -Mdir $(@) -CFLAGS "$(CFLAGS)" make -C $(@) -f V$(@)_top.mk clean:: rm -rf *.o $(TARGET) distclean:: clean rm -rf *~ *.txt *.vcd *.mif *.orig ``` ``` [phung@localhost bench]$ make distclean && make rm -rf *.o Tx rm -rf *~ *.txt *.vcd *.mif *.orig verilator -Wno-fatal -I../rtl/ --debug --trace-dups --gdbbt --gdb "valgrind -v --leak-check=yes" --cc Tx_top.v TxUART.v baud_generator.v shift_register.v --trace --exe ../Tx.cpp -Mdir Tx -CFLAGS "-g -O3" No stack. gdb /usr/bin/verilator_bin_dbg -ex 'run -Wno-fatal -I../rtl/ --debug --trace-dups --gdbbt --gdb 'valgrind -v --leak-check=yes' --cc Tx_top.v TxUART.v baud_generator.v shift_register.v --trace --exe ../Tx.cpp -Mdir Tx -CFLAGS '-g -O3'' -ex 'set width 0' -ex 'bt' gdb: unrecognized option '--leak-check=yes --cc Tx_top.v TxUART.v baud_generator.v shift_register.v --trace --exe ../Tx.cpp -Mdir Tx -CFLAGS -g' Use `gdb --help' for a complete list of options. %Error: export VERILATOR_ROOT= %Error: gdb /usr/bin/verilator_bin_dbg -ex 'run -Wno-fatal -I../rtl/ --debug --trace-dups --gdbbt --gdb 'valgrind -v --leak-check=yes' --cc Tx_top.v TxUART.v baud_generator.v shift_register.v --trace --exe ../Tx.cpp -Mdir Tx -CFLAGS '-g -O3'' -ex 'set width 0' -ex 'bt' %Error: Command Failed gdb /usr/bin/verilator_bin_dbg -ex 'run -Wno-fatal -I../rtl/ --debug --trace-dups --gdbbt --gdb 'valgrind -v --leak-check=yes' --cc Tx_top.v TxUART.v baud_generator.v shift_register.v --trace --exe ../Tx.cpp -Mdir Tx -CFLAGS '-g -O3'' -ex 'set width 0' -ex 'bt' make: *** [Makefile:14: Tx] Error 1 ```
